overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. His LinkedIn presence has the largest following, while his Bluesky activity appears to be the most recent. Overall engagement seems decent but could be improved through strategic content optimization.

contentThemes.description: Research on computational methods for social media analysis.
Detection and analysis of disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior.
Announcements of research grants, postdoc positions, and PhD opportunities.
Sharing information about upcoming conference presentations.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency, especially on platforms with lower engagement.
Use visuals (images, videos) to improve engagement.
Actively engage with comments and replies to foster a community.
Tailor content to each platform's audience and format.
Use relevant hashtags to increase discoverability.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is used to share research updates, conference announcements, and research opportunities, generating a good number of reposts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon posts focus on research releases and methodological discussions, with moderate engagement.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is primarily used to announce research grants and hiring opportunities, achieving high likes and comments.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
